zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard John Massey Sr.於1661年8月13日出生在英國爾伯頓時，他的父親Edward Massey48歲，母親Alice Braithwait37歲。他和Sarah Francis Greene至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。他於1661年住在英國英格蘭爾伯頓。他去世於1740年6月5日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州布盧斯維其可享年78歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州布盧斯維其可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard John Massey Sr.于1661年8月13日出生在英国爾伯頓时，他的父亲Edward Massey48岁，母亲Alice Braithwait37岁。他和Sarah Francis Greene至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。他于1661年住在英国英格兰爾伯頓。他去世于1740年6月5日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州布盧斯維其可享年78岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州布盧斯維其可。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).
English: variant of Marsay (see Marcey ).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massie
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeen): variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Marsee
meanings: English: altered form of   Massey  .

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Germain.
Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .
Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Masey
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

variants.name: Macy
meanings: English: variant of   Massey  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard John Massey Sr. was born on 13 August 1661, in Burton, Cheshire, England, his father, Edward Massey, was 48 and his mother, Alice Braithwait, was 37. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Sarah Francis Greene. He lived in Burton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom in 1661. He died on 5 June 1740, in Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 78, and was buried in Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard John Massey Sr. est né le 13 août 1661, à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Edward Massey, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Alice Braithwait, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Sarah Francis Greene. Il a vécu à Burton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1661. Il est décédé le 5 juin 1740, à Brunswick, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Brunswick, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard John Massey Sr. è nato il il 13 agosto 1661 nel Burton, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Edward Massey, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Alice Braithwait, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Sarah Francis Greene. Vissuto a Burton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1661. Morì il 5 giugno 1740, nel Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolto a Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard John Massey Sr.が1661年8月13日にイギリスチェシュアイアブルトンで生まれた時，父Edward Masseyは48歳，母Alice Braithwaitは37歳であった。彼はSarah Francis Greeneとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1661年にはイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアブルトンに住んでいた。彼は1740年6月5日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ブルンスウィックで78歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ブルンスウィックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard John Massey Sr.(이)가 1661년 8월 13일 영국 체실르 버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Massey(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Braithwait(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Sarah Francis Greene(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1661년도에 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버턴에서 살았다. 그는 1740년 6월 5일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 브런스윅에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 브런스윅에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard John Massey Sr. nasceu em 13 de agosto de 1661, em Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Massey, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Alice Braithwait, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Sarah Francis Greene. Ele viveu em Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1661. Ele faleceu em 5 de junho de 1740, em Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard John Massey Sr. родился 13 августа 1661 г. в местности буртон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Massey было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Braithwait, 37. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Sarah Francis Greene. Он жил в местности буртон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в 1661 году. Он умер 5 июня 1740 г. в местности брунсвикк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности брунсвикк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard John Massey Sr. nació el 13 de agosto de 1661, en Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edward Massey, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Alice Braithwait, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Sarah Francis Greene. Vivió en Burton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1661. Murió el 5 de junio de 1740, en Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Brunswick, Virginia, British Colonial America.
